title: This SEC Announcement Could Ignite Altcoin Season
transcript: The  SEC  just  released  a  rule  update  that   could  bring  in  institutional  capital   into  altcoins  for  the  first  time  in   history.  Since  I've  gotten  to  crypto  in   2017,  this  industry  has  been  waiting  for   clear  rules  that  will  make  builders  and   investors  100%  confident  in  what  they   can  and  cannot  do  in  crypto.  And  of   course,  we've  been  waiting  for  the   Clarity  Act,  which  formalizes  this  into   law  at  the  highest  level.  But  the  next   best  thing  is  the  actual  enforcement   agency,  the  SEC,  coming  out  with  one  of   the  most  historic  and  bullish  rule   updates  for  crypto  in  history.  And  this   is  not  by  a  small  margin.  This  is  by   miles.  Today  was  historic  and  in  my   opinion  sets  the  foundation  for  the  next   altcoin  run  backed  by  institutional   capital.  Listen  to  SEC  Chair  Paul   Atkins.  So  today,  I'm  pleased  to   announce  that  the  SEC's  persistent   failure  to  provide  clarity  on  this   question  is  over.  As  we  speak,  the   commission  is  implementing  a  token   taxonomy  and  investment  contract   interpretation  and  you  should  find  that   in  your  inbox  or  if  you  look  on  the   website  when  you  get  home.  Our   interpretation,  which  is  grounded  in   existing  law  and  informed  by  extensive   public  input,  establishes  four  asset   categories  that  are  not  deemed  to  be   securities.  So,  digital  commodities,   digital  collectibles,  digital  tools,  and   payment  stable  coins  under  the  Dee   Genius  Act.  With  these  categories  in   place,  the  interpretation  then  clarifies   that  only  one  crypto  asset  class  remains   subject  to  securities  laws,  namely   digital  securities,  which  are   traditional  securities  that  are   tokenized.  So,  this  is  huge.  Absolutely   everything  in  crypto  has  no  fear,  no   worry  of  being  enforced  upon  as  a   security  under  these  new  guidelines,   unless  you're  tokenizing  a  stock,  a   literal  security,  which  is,  you  know,   akin  to  meta  stock.  If  you're  trying  to   sell  that  on  the  blockchain,  now  you  got   to  deal  with  securities  laws.  Why?   Because  those  are  already  governed  by   securities  laws.  But  the  new  token   ecosystems  have  none  of  that.  This  is   absolutely  insane.  We'll  talk  about  all   the  knock-on  effects  of  this  in  just  a   second.  This  distinction  returns  the  SEC   to  its  core  mission  and  statutory   authority  of  protecting  investors   involved  in  securities  transactions.   We're  not  the  Securities  and  Everything   Commission  anymore.  So,  thank  you.   >> The  Securities  and  Everything  Commission   was  of  course  a  tongue  and  cheek  name   for  the  SEC  which  had  started  to  go  way   beyond  its  statutory  guidelines  of   protecting  investors  around  investment   contracts.  meaning  if  there  was  an   investment  contract,  if  there  was  a   regulated  asset,  then  they  should  come   in  and  do  something.  But  just  going  in   wherever  anyone  has  transacted  money  for   anything  that  could  potentially  go  up  or   down  in  value,  that's  wrong.  If  you  go   to  a  garage  sale  and  you  buy  some   Pokémon  cards,  the  SEC  doesn't  belong  in   that  transaction.  You  shouldn't  have   reporting  requirements,  right?  This  is  a   return  to  common  sense.  And  what  it   really  does  is  it  opens  up  the  floodgate   for  institutional  capital  that  was   simply  never  going  to  buy  these  assets   until  they  knew  for  sure  that  they  would   never  get  regulated  out  of  existence.   That's  something  that  institutional   capital  will  never  do.  It  will  never   take  a  risk  on  an  asset  going  absolutely   to  zero  due  to  regulatory  risk.  Here  is   the  full  ruling  on  the  official  SEC   website.  It  says  that  it  clarifies  the   application  of  federal  securities  laws.   And  in  my  opinion,  the  real  mic  drop   line  was,  and  with  today's   interpretation,  the  wait  is  over  for  the   crypto  industry.  It  provides  a  clear  and   coherent  token  taxonomy  for  digital   commodities,  collectibles,  tools,  and   stable  coins,  and  effectively  anything   that's  not  a  digital  equity  or  a  digital   stock,  right?  A  digital  security.  We   also  got  a  massive  bonus  here,  which  was   the  CFTC  saying  that  non-custodial   wallet  providers  can  absolutely   facilitate  trades  and  they  do  not  need   to  be  worried  about  having  some  kind  of   enforcement  action  or  being  considered   in  violation  of  the  bank  secrecy  act  or   some  kind  of  other  regulated  banking   activity.  This  is  massive  because  we  all   know  that  permissionless  finance  totally   relies  on  not  having  to  KYC  and  manage   the  activities  and  identities  of  the   customers  that  interact  with  code.  And   this  clarifies  that.  You  can  see  Phantom   obviously  having  themselves  a  field  day   below.  In  my  opinion,  this  was  the   missing  piece.  This  was  the  foundational   element  that  was  keeping  the  big  boys  on   the  sidelines.  And  this  also  stopped   builders  from  doing  common  sense  things   like  having  some  kind  of  value  flow   through  from  the  token  ecosystem  to  the   token  holders  themselves.  For  example,   the  way  that  protocols  actually  create   value  for  their  tokens  is  doing   buybacks.  And  the  tokens  that  have  value   flow  directly  to  token  holders  have  been   in  serious  stressful  situations  where  a   lot  of  people  believed  that  was  just   going  to  be  a  security  that  was  just   going  to  get  regulated  out  of  existence.   In  this  case,  we  now  know  that  tokens   can  be  treated  like  equity  in  stocks.  So   if  the  stock  goes  up  or  creates  value,  a   dividend  can  be  paid.  For  example,   Hyperlquid  token  holders  could  very  soon   actually  get  the  fees  from  Hyperlquid.   Instead  of  those  just  going  to  a  private   company  that  then  chooses  to  do  buybacks   with  the  tokens  that  keeps  the  price   high,  there  very  well  could  just  be  a   dividend  paid  to  the  token  holders  and   then  the  token  would  normalize  at   whatever  price  token  holders  are  willing   to  pay  for  that  dividend.  That  is  like   being  an  owner  in  the  protocol.  That  is   the  actual  holy  grail.  The  dream  of  a   token  holder  is  to  be  treated  like  an   equity  holder,  like  an  owner.  And  that   is  what  can  happen  now  that  we  have   these  clear  rules.  It  is  magnificent.  It   is  incredible.  And  in  my  opinion,  it   shows  that  we  are  moving  towards  common   sense  legislation  for  crypto  and  that   it's  probably  going  to  get  done  very   soon.  And  that  in  the  midst  of  this  year   being  a  bare  market  year,  the  chance  to   pick  up  discount  cryptos  that  will  have   all  the  rights  and  all  of  the  privileges   of  equity  holders  in  early  stage   ventures.  That's  the  dream.  And  that   will,  in  my  opinion,  form  the  foundation   of  the  altcoin  bull  run,  the  giga  alt   season  of  tomorrow  that  will  make  even   the  mega  cap  bull  runs  of  the  2010s  and   2020s  absolutely  blush.  So  what  are  some   of  these  tokens  that  could  do  very  very   well?  Well,  in  my  opinion,  top  of  the   list  is  Hyperlquid.  Nothing  makes  as   much  money  right  now  as  Hyperlid.  And   the  ability  for  those  token  holders  to   start  directly  participating  in  the   profits  is  absolutely  incredible.  Of   course,  there  doesn't  have  to  be  a  100%   fee  to  paid  to  token  holders.  There   could  be  some  set  apart  for  development.   It  doesn't  have  to  all  be  one  to  one,   but  the  point  is  now  treating  your  token   holders  like  real  owners  is  going  to  be   allowed.  That  is  incredible.  The  other   tokens  I  could  see  doing  really  really   well  are  potentially  tokens  like  Salana,   which  we  all  know  that  there's  network   fees  that  are  paid  for  gas.  Right  now,   those  fees  are  usually  burned  in  the   case  of  Ethereum,  but  what  if  they  were   paid  through  to  the  token  holders  or   stakers?  That's  a  very  interesting  value   proposition  that  actually  holding  the   token  can  have  a  revenue  model  in  and  of   itself.  Something  like  these  layer  1   ones.  Now,  one  that  I  would  absolutely   love  to  see  this  happen  with  is  Pump.   Fun.  Pump.  Fun  is  largely  considered  one   of  the  most  extractive  projects  in  the   history  of  crypto  because  they  just  take   all  the  fees.  Now,  they  have  been  doing   a  lot  of  buybacks  with  their  token,  but   it  hasn't  been  able  to  move  the  price   much.  Being  able  to  root  the  actual   revenue  from  Pump.  Fund  right  into  the   pump  token.  I  think  this  is  another   clear  example  that  you  could  actually   make  the  case  that  pump.fund  actually  is   a  very  valuable  token  and  that  there   wouldn't  have  to  be  this  mysterious   layer  between  the  buybacks  and  the  token   holders  and  that  you  could  see  some  real   excitement  and  I  think  it  could  reverse   one  of  the  most  stigmatized  tokens  in   the  industry.  Pump.fund  or  really  any   massive  revenue  generating  token  stands   to  benefit  tremendously  from  this.   Another  DeFi  giant,  Jupiter,  obviously  a   sponsor  of  the  channel.  Shout  out  to   them.  They  have  a  new  giveaway  where  you   can  win  up  to  $2  million.  Uh  check  out   their  trade  eligible  spot  payers,  their   referring  friends.  And  essentially,  this   is  a  another  way  that  they're  giving   back  to  people  actually  using  their   platform.  Again,  this  wouldn't  need  to   happen.  They  wouldn't  need  to  go  through   all  of  this  with  these  new  regulations   if  they  were  confident  that  they  could   just  simply  give  token  holders  the  same   rights  as  equity  holders.  And  I  think   that's  the  future.  I  think  that  as  we   look  at  the  altcoin  market,  you're  going   to  see  more  and  more  the  treating  of   these  tokens  as  fullcale  equity   participants  as  big  boys  at  the  table.   And  that  the  tokens  that  are  enshrined   with  the  most  rights  in  the  community,   those  are  the  ones  that  are  going  to   thrive.  The  ones  with  the  very   interesting  use  cases  where  as  the   network  succeeds,  they  succeed.  A  great   example  of  this  would  have  been  OpenC,   right,  which  just  recently  today  decided   not  to  launch  their  token.  But  if  they   had  launched  a  token  with  a  fee  sharing   model,  the  thing  would  have  been  one  of   the  biggest  tokens  in  the  world  at  one   point,  of  course,  before  NFTs  died.  I   think  there's  going  to  be  some  really   interesting  design  space  with   decentralized  infrastructure  being  used   to  train  AI  being  leveraged  in  the   crypto  space  and  with  the  profits  and   the  business  model  decentralized.  That   is  going  to  be  a  space  that  I'm  going to   be  looking  at  incredibly  closely  now   that  we  have  the  go-ahehead  to  treat   token  holders  like  owners,  seeing  value   flow  into  decentralized  networks  to   train  AIS,  which  we  know  there's  insane   demand  for,  and  seeing  the  value  of  that   actually  be  distributed  to  token   holders.  This  is  the  most  interesting   time  to  be  an  investor,  not  only  just  in   crypto,  but  also  in  the  world.  The  speed   of  change,  the  amount  of  new  things   happening,  the  amount  of  opportunity   here  in  my  opinion  will  be  looked  back   at  as  the  most  opportunistic  time  in   history.  Despite  that,  we  have  some  of   the  most  negative,  downtrodden,   absolutely  abysmal  sentiment  in  the   investment  world  in  history  and  just  in   the  world.  I  think  has  everything  to  do   with  people  just  being  too  hyperfixated   on  the  negative  side  of  things  on  social   media.  It's  another  topic,  but  I  do   believe  that  this  ruling  today  will  set   off  the  altcoin  giga  cycle.  Not  just   today,  but  obviously  over  the  coming   years.  This  is  a  long-term  change.   Doesn't  mean  that  things  go  up  and  to   the  right  today,  but  it  means  that  over   the  next  few  cycles,  we  will  see  not   just  speculative  value,  but  real  value   creation  on  chain.  And  it  is  going  to  be   my  great  pleasure  to  be  here  and  to   steer  the  ship  as  we  all  benefit  from   this  together.  If  you  enjoyed  this   video,  make  sure  to  click  on  this  one   popping  up  right  now  as  I'm  sure  you'll   love  it.  And  of  course,  make  sure  to   like  and  subscribe.  Liking  this  video  is   super  important  to  do  in  these  bearish   times.  I  truly  appreciate  it.  It's  free   and  easy  to  do.  It's  a  bare  market.  We   need  all  the  likes  and  the  clicks.  And  I   truly  appreciate  you  for  stopping  by.   and  I'll  see  you  very  soon  on  the  next
summary_hu: Az SEC új szabályfrissítése jelentős áttörést jelent az intézményi tőke beáramlásában az alternatív kriptovalutákba. A frissítések tisztázzák a kriptovaluták besorolását négy kategóriába, amelyek nem minősülnek értékpapíroknak, így csökkentik a szabályozási kockázatot. Az SEC az új irányelvekkel visszatér a befektetők védelméhez, és megszünteti a korábbi mindenre kiterjedő megközelítést. Az elemző úgy véli, hogy ez megnyitja az utat az intézményi tőke számára, így új lehetőségeket teremt az altcoin piacon. A szabályozói környezet világossá teszi, hogy a nyereségmegosztás a token birtokosok számára is lehetővé válik, ami innovatív váltás a kriptopiacon. A hagyományos értékpapírokhoz való hasonlóság pozitív hatással lesz a piaci érzéseket illetően, ezáltal a következő altcoin ciklus fellendülését eredményezheti.
summary_en: The SEC's new rule update represents a significant breakthrough for institutional capital entering altcoins. The updates categorize cryptocurrencies into four sections that are not considered securities, thus reducing regulatory risk. The SEC is refocusing on investor protection, moving away from its previous overreaching approach. The analyst believes this opens the door for institutional capital, creating new opportunities in the altcoin market. The clarified regulatory environment allows profit-sharing models for token holders, marking an innovative shift in crypto. The analogy to traditional securities will positively influence market sentiment, potentially leading to the next altcoin cycle of growth.
key_points_hu: Az SEC új szabályozása csökkenti a kripto piacra nehezedő szabályozási nyomást.
Az intézményi tőke előtérbe kerülhet, ami új lehetőségeket teremt az altcoinok számára.
A token birtokosok profitmegosztási lehetőséget kapnak, ami innovatív változásokhoz vezethet.
key_points_en: The SEC's new regulations reduce regulatory pressure on the crypto market.
Institutional capital could gain prominence, creating new opportunities for altcoins.
Token holders are granted profit-sharing opportunities, potentially leading to innovative changes.
